headline: Wells Fargo Money Study Recasts American Dream And Future Growth Drivers
summary: Wells Fargo (NYSE:WFC) released its 2026 Money Study, highlighting how Americans are redefining financial success and the American Dream. The research points to growing interest in entrepreneurship and multigenerational family support as core parts of financial plans. The study also highlights changing views on financial advice, delayed life milestones, and the role of business ownership, especially among younger adults. For you as an investor watching NYSE:WFC, this research sits at the...

headline: Apollo to Expand U.S. Footprint With Second Headquarters Plan
summary: APO explores a second U.S. HQ in South Florida or Texas to tap high-growth markets, expand client reach, boost efficiency and support long-term AUM growth.

headline: Uber Price Target Cut by Wells Fargo to $95 Due to Autonomous Vehicle Impact
summary: Uber Technologies (NYSE:UBER) has had a rough start to 2026. Shares are down 16.59% year-to-date, off 7.23% over the past week and sit 5.15% below where they were one year ago. The stock touched a 52-week high of $101.99 but has since retreated to $69.11. Most analysts cluster around a consensus 12-month price target of ... headline: Wells Fargo 2026 Money Study Reveals Americans Redefining the American Dream; Gen Z leaning on Parents for Financial Support
summary: SAN FRANCISCO, March 30, 2026--Americans are rethinking what financial success looks like, how they get advice, and how parents are providing financial support to adult children, according to the 2026 Wells Fargo Money Study. The research shows a shift toward entrepreneurship as a form of control and independence, growing use of artificial intelligence for financial guidance, and increasing financial strain and communication challenges between parents and Gen Z adults.

headline: Big Tech Stocks Rout Is Flashing Signals of a Turnaround
summary: (Bloomberg) -- The wreckage in large technology stocks that sent the Nasdaq 100 Index into a correction is flashing signs that have marked turning points for the group in the past.Chief among the signals is how much of Big Tech’s valuation premium to the rest of the market has been erased. Such a compression has historically set the sector up for outperformance.After an 11% slide from its last record in October, the tech-focused gauge now trades at 21 times projected 12-month earnings, just 1.7
